confirmed.summary: WP03 operationalizes 'catches exactly what the gates catch' (set-equality parity) as merely 'both catch one planted violation' — a strictly weaker, over-flag-blind check that a trivially-over-flagging driver passes
confirmed.evidence: WP03-parity-prove-literal-sweeps.md:41 states the goal as 'the driver's detection set == the set the old enforcing sweeps flag' but the concrete DoD (line 42, DoD line 45) only requires 'construct a fixture with a planted violation of each; assert both the driver AND the old sweep catch it'. Spec SC-003 demands the driver 'catches exactly what those ... gates catch' (spec.md:73). Catching one planted violation proves neither no-over-flag nor no-under-flag, so the objectively-checkable DoD is weaker than the stated success criterion.
confirmed.recommendation: Strengthen the WP03 DoD to a real set comparison over shared input: (1) planted-violation fixture → both flag it; (2) a benign-but-similar-looking control input → both ignore it (proves no over-flag); (3) run both against the same fixture tree and assert equal detection sets. Also note the feasibility trap the DoD ignores: `test_no_legacy_terminology.py::_grep_for` hardcodes `_repo_root()` (git grep over the real tree, line 89-101) and `test_no_legacy_path_literals.py` hardcodes `root = .../src/specify_cli/cli` (line 63) — neither exposes a fixture-injection seam, so 'run the old sweep against a planted-violation fixture' requires monkeypatching/subprocess-in-fabricated-repo. Without that, both the parity check and the 'still-blocking' assertion degrade to a source-grep for the string 'pytest.fail', which is trivially fakeable.

verdict.reasoning:
The finding withstands adversarial scrutiny against the live artifacts and code.

VERIFIED SUBSTANCE: SC-003 (spec.md:73) verbatim demands the driver "catches exactly what the ... sweeps catch" (bidirectional set-equality). WP03 (tasks/WP03-parity-prove-literal-sweeps.md:41) operationalizes this as "construct a fixture with a planted violation of each; assert both the driver AND the old sweep catch it" — an existence check on a violation-only fixture that a trivially-over-flagging driver passes, proving neither no-over-flag nor no-under-flag. The gap survives the task's stronger "==" language (lines 45, 50): the SPECIFIED input is still a violation-only fixture, so even a real set `==` over it is over-flag-blind (no benign content, no probe of the scan_roots/exemptions envelope). design.md:161 explicitly names over-broad-sweep false-friction as the central risk and design.md:102 notes the driver's exemptions "replaces each test's hand-rolled exclusion list" — the exact divergence vector a violation-only fixture cannot detect. The finding's recommended benign-control + shared-tree comparison genuinely closes it and appears nowhere in the artifacts (not already-handled).

VERIFIED FEASIBILITY TRAP: test_no_legacy_terminology.py::_grep_for hardcodes _repo_root() with git grep over the live tree (lines 89-101), pytest.fail at 124, no injectable seam. test_no_legacy_path_literals.py's in-scope CLI-grep half hardcodes root=.../src/specify_cli/cli at line 63 with inline logic (64-77), no seam — while the out-of-scope global-state check DID get an injectable _scan_global_state_for_home_literal(repo_root, ...) seam (line 221), sharpening the point. The task (line 42) explicitly permits the weaker "import + introspect" fallback that degrades to a source-grep for 'pytest.fail', trivially fakeable.

Mission is at tasks stage (status.json event_count 0, no parity test exists), so this is a legitimate pre-implementation planning-quality finding, not stale.

Minor citation imprecision: the finding attributes the parenthetical quote to "line 42, DoD line 45" but it is on line 41, and it says line 45 "only requires" the existence check when line 45 actually says "==". These slips do not defeat the argument (quoted strings exist verbatim; the over-flag-blindness of the violation-only fixture holds regardless).

confirmed.summary: Seed-record count is stated inconsistently (one vs two), and WP01 solely owns the manifest WP03 depends on — under-seeding leaves WP03 unable to run its path-literal parity
confirmed.evidence: spec.md:43 FR-004: 'seed the manifest with **one** already-caught contract'; tasks.md:20 T003: 'Seed ONE `retired_literal` record (from `test_no_legacy_terminology.py`)'. But WP01 tasks file line 59 says 'Seed the manifest with the `retired_literal` records for `test_no_legacy_terminology.py` ... + the CLI-tree literal-grep half of `test_no_legacy_path_literals.py`' (two), and WP03 line 40 asserts 'The literal-sweep records (seeded in WP01) exist for the terminology terms + the CLI-tree path literals.' WP01 owned_files is the sole owner of docs/contracts/contract-registry.yaml (WP01-...md:32); WP03 owned_files is only tests/architectural/test_contract_registry_parity.py, so WP03 cannot add the path-literal record itself.
confirmed.recommendation: Reconcile the count: make WP01 T003 seed BOTH records (terminology + CLI-tree path-literal) and update FR-004 + tasks.md T003 + the WP01 DoD/acceptance-matrix to say two, since WP01 is the only WP that can write the manifest and WP03/FR-006's parity depends on the path-literal record already existing. Alternatively, add docs/contracts/contract-registry.yaml to WP03's editable surface — but the single-owner approach is cleaner.

verdict.reasoning:
VERDICT: REAL (medium, boundary). Every evidentiary claim verifies exactly against the live planning artifacts, and the boundary consequence holds. Implementation has NOT started (status.json event_count 0; docs/contracts/, src/specify_cli/contracts/, the driver, and the parity test are all MISSING), so this is a genuine post-tasks planning review — the planning docs themselves are the artifact, and the inconsistency lives in them right now.

EXACT-QUOTE VERIFICATION (all six citations match live text):
- spec.md:43 FR-004 — "seed the manifest with **one** already-caught contract" (ONE). Confirmed.
- tasks.md:20 T003 — "Seed ONE `retired_literal` record (from `test_no_legacy_terminology.py`)..." (ONE, terminology only). Confirmed verbatim.
- WP01 line 59 — "Seed the manifest with the `retired_literal` records for `test_no_legacy_terminology.py` ... + the CLI-tree literal-grep half of `test_no_legacy_path_literals.py` ... (These feed WP02's driver + WP03's parity.)" (TWO). Confirmed verbatim.
- WP03 line 40 — "The literal-sweep records (seeded in WP01) exist for the terminology terms + the CLI-tree path literals." (WP03 depends on BOTH being seeded in WP01). Confirmed verbatim.
- WP01 owned_files line 32 — "docs/contracts/contract-registry.yaml". Confirmed WP01 is the manifest owner.
- WP03 owned_files line 24 — only "tests/architectural/test_contract_registry_parity.py". Confirmed WP03 cannot write the manifest.

The inconsistency is not isolated — it is systemic and I corroborated it beyond the finding's own citations: FOUR surfaces say ONE (design.md lines 142/169/176 "single"/"one" record; spec FR-004; plan.md IC-01 "Seed one record (FR-004)"; plan.md Project Structure comment "# IC-01 NEW manifest (seeded with 1 record)"; tasks.md T003 index+body) while the parity path needs TWO (spec FR-006 models terminology + path-literal as records; plan.md IC-03; tasks.md T005; WP01 line 59; WP03 line 40). Grep confirms NO WP other than WP01 references or owns contract-registry.yaml — WP02 owns only the driver, WP03 only the parity test. So WP03 structurally cannot seed the path-literal record it depends on.

ROOT CAUSE (adds signal): design.md line 142 describes an "introduce-then-adopt, never big-bang" model where WP1 seeds a single record and "Each subsequent WP adopts one slice's consumer sets into records" — i.e., later WPs were meant to write records into the manifest. The tasks-phase then assigned the manifest solely to WP01, breaking that assumption for WP03. WP01's guidance line 59 half-patched this by telling WP01 to seed both, but FR-004, plan IC-01, plan's Project Structure comment, and tasks.md T003 were never updated to match — a classic DIR-032 conceptual-alignment failure (two artifacts that must agree, disagree) inside a mission whose entire subject is contract-ownership boundaries.

ADVERSARIAL REFUTATIONS, ALL FAIL:
1) "WP01 line 59 already says seed both, so no real risk" — only a partial mitigation. WP01's requirement_refs are FR-001..FR-004 (NOT FR-006); FR-004 + plan IC-01 + plan "1 record" + tasks.md T003 all say ONE. An implementer/reviewer verifying WP01 against its accountable requirement (FR-004) can legitimately ship one record and pass, especially once the acceptance-matrix FR-004 criterion (currently a TODO placeholder) is authored to check "one." Line 59 is the lone dissenting surface buried in guidance.
2) "one vs two is additive, not contradictory" — refuted: tasks.md T003 explicitly scopes the seed to terminology and excludes path-literal; plan's Project Structure says "1 record" flat; WP03 says records were "seeded in WP01." That is a contradictory count, not a clean additive split.
3) "WP03 can build its own in-fixture record" — refuted by WP03 line 40 (records are "seeded in WP01") + ownership: an in-fixture record would not prove parity of the actual owned manifest record, and WP03 cannot write the manifest.
4) "stale docs" — refuted: files mtime today 20:22, read live, all quotes exact.

SEVERITY: medium is correct. Not low — if WP01 follows the ONE instruction, WP03 is un-completable without a boundary violation (WP03 editing WP01's owned manifest) and cannot self-remediate; this is the exact DIR-001/DIR-032 boundary-integrity class the mission exists to prevent. Not high — caught pre-implementation, no shipped harm, and the fix is a documentation reconciliation. The finding's recommendation (make WP01 T003 seed BOTH and update FR-004 + tasks.md T003 + WP01 DoD/acceptance-matrix to two, since WP01 is the sole manifest writer and FR-006/WP03 parity needs the path-literal record) is the correct single-owner fix and matches intent already latent in WP01 line 59 and FR-006; it correctly deprioritizes the alternative of widening WP03's editable surface.

confirmed.summary: WP02's advisory sweep has no positive assertion that the REAL seeded registry is non-vacuously wired — the anti-vacuity control only exercises a synthetic fixture, so a live sweep that loads zero retired records is a silent green
confirmed.evidence: WP02-absence-sweep-driver.md:38 the anti-vacuity control 'plants a reappearance ... in a fixture/temp tree'; DoD line 41 'The sweep runs over the seeded status=retired records ... it never blocks CI on a live-tree find (advisory)'. Because the driver is report-only on live finds AND the anti-vacuity control builds its own fixture, nothing reds if `load_registry` returns zero retired records (mis-seeded status, loader bug). 'The sweep runs over the seeded records' is therefore not a pass/fail-checkable DoD.
confirmed.recommendation: Add a positive wiring assertion distinct from the advisory report and the fixture control: assert the driver enumerates >=1 `status=retired` record from the real `docs/contracts/contract-registry.yaml` and actually swept its declared scan_roots (e.g. assert the swept-file count > 0). This guards against a silently-empty live sweep — the exact DIR-041 vacuity the mission exists to prevent.

verdict.reasoning:
CORE CLAIM SURVIVES ADVERSARIAL REFUTATION AGAINST LIVE ARTIFACTS. Both quotes are verbatim-accurate: WP02-absence-sweep-driver.md:38 scopes the anti-vacuity control to "a fixture/temp tree" and explicitly to "the driver's own correctness, NOT a live-tree retirement"; line 41's DoD reads "The sweep runs over the seeded status=retired records ... it never blocks CI on a live-tree find (advisory)"; line 37 confirms report-only. The finding's mechanism is sound: (a) the sweep never reds on a live find (advisory), and (b) the anti-vacuity control tests detection on a synthetic tree, so a live sweep enumerating zero retired records (mis-seeded status, loader bug, or scan_roots resolving to nothing) yields the same green as a clean sweep. DoD line 41 "runs over the seeded records" is thus satisfiable by a test that would also pass against an empty enumeration — genuinely not runtime pass/fail-checkable.

NOT ALREADY HANDLED. I checked every sibling gate: WP01's enforcing `doctor contracts` (FR-002) validates well-formedness on a DIFFERENT surface (a status=active mis-seed is well-formed and passes; it never asserts the WP02 driver enumerated >=1 record or swept >0 files). WP02's anti-vacuity control is spec-scoped away from the live-tree axis (line 38). WP03's parity (line 41) also builds its own fixture; only line 40 "records ... exist" touches the live registry, covering at most the enumeration half and never the swept-file-count>0 half, and it lives downstream in WP03. design.md:179 "The WP1-seeded record proves it bites" is prose intent, not an enforced assertion — it actually reinforces the gap (nothing reds if that intent silently goes false). The finding is also new (not among the seven prior post-plan.json verdicts).

STEELMANNED DEFENSES FAIL ON SUBSTANCE: "advisory so harmless" only bounds impact — NFR-002 explicitly forbids "a green 'verified' that hides a break," which a silently-empty absence sweep is; "the control already prevents vacuous green" conflates two vacuity axes (mechanism vs live wiring) that the spec's own line 38 separates; "doctor gate covers it" is a distinct well-formedness surface blind to status/scan-root/enumeration regressions. The recommendation (a self-test asserting >=1 status=retired enumerated and swept-file count > 0, distinct from the advisory report) is coherent and does NOT contradict the advisory-on-finds design.

WHY SEVERITY DOWNGRADED medium->low: (1) The sweep is advisory in v1 (SC-002 "never blocks"), so a vacuous green degrades a missing WARNING, not enforcement — an active break is not introduced. (2) Partial mitigation exists via WP01 doctor validation + WP03's "records exist" for the enumeration half. (3) The finding overclaims by branding this "the exact DIR-041 vacuity the mission exists to prevent" — DIR-041 ("Tests as Scaffold, Not Friction", design.md:6/121) is about single-source-of-truth + content-anchoring/no-file:line rot, not empty-sweep vacuity; the mission's raison d'etre is hand-synced-copy rot, so the DIR-041 framing is a rhetorical overreach peripheral to the valid core. This matches this mission's own calibration: the sibling advisory-scope finding in post-plan.json (exemption-narrowness guard) was rated low with identical "advisory in v1 / meta-guard not active break" reasoning. Net: a genuine, live, non-stale, unhandled DoD-hardening gap — real=true at low severity.

confirmed.summary: Grounding mischaracterizes the composition of test_no_legacy_path_literals.py: it enumerates 'CLI-tree literal-grep half' + 'behavioral tests', omitting a second literal-grep gate that is neither
confirmed.evidence: spec.md:14 states path_literals splits into the CLI-tree literal-grep half plus 'behavioral tests (mock `SPEC_KITTY_HOME`, invoke `_emit_migrate_nudge`, assert stderr)'. But tests/audit/test_no_legacy_path_literals.py:246 also defines test_no_handrolled_spec_kitty_home_in_global_state_modules — a second static literal-grep (over src/specify_cli + src/kernel for bare '.spec-kitty' home literals, FR-010) that is neither the CLI-tree grep nor a behavioral nudge test.
confirmed.recommendation: Add the global-state literal-grep to the explicit carve-out list (it is a single-source `.spec-kitty` home guard, not a retired-literal, so correctly out of scope) so a reviewer verifying the fold does not mistake it for an in-scope literal sweep or assume the file was fully enumerated. Precise WP03 anchoring to line 74 means no implementation change, hence low severity.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified against the live main checkout (kitty-specs/contract-ownership-boundary-01KWYRE5/spec.md and tests/audit/test_no_legacy_path_literals.py). The finding survives adversarial scrutiny on every factual claim.

CONFIRMED: (1) spec.md:14 decomposes test_no_legacy_path_literals.py into exactly two parts — the "CLI-tree literal-grep half" (in scope) + "behavioral tests (mock SPEC_KITTY_HOME, invoke _emit_migrate_nudge, assert stderr)" (out of scope), quoted verbatim. (2) The file at line 246 defines test_no_handrolled_spec_kitty_home_in_global_state_modules, a distinct static literal-grep that is NEITHER of those two: it scans different roots (_GLOBAL_STATE_SCAN_ROOTS = src/specify_cli + src/kernel, line 184 — not src/specify_cli/cli) for a different literal (bare ['\"]\.spec-kitty['\"], line 206 — not the ~/\.(kittify|spec-kitty) tilde pattern at line 56), tied to FR-010 / issue #2171. (3) The file's own docstring (lines 4-26) explicitly enumerates "Three complementary checks," so the spec's 2-way framing is objectively incomplete, not merely a matter of reading. (4) It is not already handled by the spec.md:25 carve-out, which covers only AST-directional / behavioral / directory-existence checks — the global-state home-literal grep is none of those, so it sits unmentioned (neither in-scope nor explicitly carved out).

Refutation attempts all fail: "illustrative not exhaustive" (line 14 is framed as the complete foldable/non-foldable partition — "only part is... foldable... The rest do NOT fold"); "loosely the CLI grep" (different roots + different literal, so mislabeling); "already carved out" (not AST/behavioral/directory); "immaterial so unreal" (low severity is not unreality).

Severity correctly stays LOW: WP03 (tasks/WP03-...md:36 and tasks.md:30) anchors the fold precisely to "the CLI-tree literal-grep... (assert, line ~74)" = test_no_legacy_path_literals_in_cli_commands (assert at line 74), which never touches line 246, so the omission produces zero implementation drift. This is a genuine but minor grounding-completeness/coverage gap with a specific, correct, actionable recommendation (add the single-source .spec-kitty home guard to the explicit carve-out list so a reviewer verifying the fold does not assume full enumeration or mistake it for an in-scope literal sweep). Real, low.

dropped.summary: acceptance-matrix.json is 100% placeholder for a mission whose entire thesis is DIR-041 anti-vacuity — no objective acceptance criteria and zero negative invariants
dropped.why_refuted:
The file contents the finding cites are accurate (acceptance-matrix.json is 100% TODO placeholder with negative_invariants []), but the interpretation, severity, and impact are all a misread of a canonical, expected scaffold state — so the finding does not withstand adversarial scrutiny.

1) VERBATIM AUTO-SCAFFOLD: The observed matrix is the exact output of scaffold_acceptance_matrix() (src/specify_cli/acceptance/matrix.py:215-276): one pending criterion per spec FR id with description "Verify {req_id} is satisfied", notes=SCAFFOLD_TODO_MARKER, negative_invariants=[]. It was auto-written by `spec-kitty agent mission finalize-tasks` (mission_finalize.py:1246) and committed in "Add tasks for feature contract-ownership-boundary-01KWYRE5". The docstring explicitly designs it as "valid yet obviously awaiting real content" at task-finalization time.

2) WRONG STAGE: status.events.jsonl shows the mission at post-tasks / pre-implementation (TasksCompleted 2026-07-07T18:22; WP01/02/03 just created; no WP claimed/implemented). Matrix curation is an accept-time (post-implementation) obligation — the finalize hint says "create it manually before acceptance," and the accept pipeline reads/mutates it, runs negative invariants, and computes the verdict. 29 of 96 missions in the same repo sit in the identical placeholder state; this is the normal pre-accept condition, not a mission-specific defect.

3) DIR-041 MISCHARACTERIZED: DIR-041 ("Tests as Scaffold, Not Friction") governs test anti-vacuity + content-anchoring (no file:line ratchets). The mission's DIR-041 usage (spec NFR-003) is specifically that the contract REGISTRY must not reintroduce file:line anchoring — unrelated to acceptance-matrix.json curation. The finding conflates a workflow evidence artifact with the mission's substantive thesis.

4) CRITERIA ALREADY EXIST: spec.md carries SC-001..004 (with explicit red-first negative controls: malformed AND file:line-anchored record must fail; planted reappearance flagged; advisory never blocks; parity with still-enforcing gates) plus NFR-001..004 and per-FR independent tests. The finding itself quotes SC-001/002/003, proving the objective criteria are present in their canonical home.

5) ENFORCEMENT BACKWARDS (FAIL-CLOSED): An all-placeholder matrix has every pass_fail="pending" → overall_verdict "pending" (matrix.py:135-137) → the accept gate blocks with "criteria or invariants have not been verified" (acceptance/__init__.py:1066-1067). A placeholder matrix therefore CANNOT enable vacuous acceptance; it prevents acceptance until real evidence is authored. The finding's central claim that this makes 'objectively checkable' unenforceable is the inverse of how the gate operates.

Severity adjusted from high to low: the recommendation is a routine restatement of what the accept gate already enforces automatically at the correct (post-implementation) stage; there is no defect at the post-tasks stage.

dropped.summary: C-003 'consumer sets discovered-then-frozen, never hand-typed blind' via the test_no_dead_symbols resolver cannot bind the v1 seed, which is retired_literal-kind whose 'consumer set' is a hand-copied scan_roots+exemptions — making the anti-laziness reviewer checkpoint un-satisfiable for what is actually built
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's factual scaffolding is accurate, but its load-bearing conclusion (an "un-satisfiable / vacuous" anti-laziness reviewer checkpoint) is refuted by the live artifacts. Verified true sub-claims: test_no_dead_symbols.py IS a symbol-keyed AST import-graph resolver (_resolve_import_from:960, _imports_by_target:1281, _symbol_has_caller:1321, keyed on module::Name); the v1 seed (FR-004 spec.md:43, WP01 T003:59) is retired_literal-only (no fallback_name record is seeded); and the terminology test's scope is hand-maintained constants (_SCAN_ROOTS = ("src","tests","docs") L36, _EXCLUDED_PATH_FRAGMENTS L42-58). It is also true that no import/call graph can "discover" a string literal's scan scope.

Where the finding fails:

1) It misreads C-003's resolver clause as a binding mandate on the retired_literal seed. The design cleanly scopes the resolver to the SYMBOL kinds: design.md:116 assigns test_no_dead_symbols's "(module, name) import-site" resolver to the `signature` kind as "the consumer-discovery seam"; design.md:118 assigns `retired_literal` to the test_no_legacy_* family with NO resolver. design.md:101-102 defines a retired_literal's consumer set as scan_roots (the "static-arm sweep scope") + exemptions ("replaces each test's hand-rolled exclusion list") — i.e. the sweep SCOPE, not a call-site graph. design.md:160's "discovered import/call graph" language sits under the "Wrong consumer set = false confidence" case, which is about an omitted call-site consumer (symbol), not a literal's scan scope. And design.md:200/203 place resolver-based discovery in an OPEN DECISION and tie it to the DEFERRED signature kind ("or codegraph"). So the spec's compressed C-003 one-liner is illustrative and symbol-kind-scoped, not a mandate the retired_literal seed violates.

2) The reviewer checkpoint IS satisfiable and checkable. "Discovered (not hand-typed blind)" for a literal means derived from the superseded test's proven scan scope, then frozen/reviewed — exactly what T003 directs ("scan_roots + exemptions mirroring the old tests' scope"). A reviewer confirms the seeded values mirror _SCAN_ROOTS/_EXCLUDED_PATH_FRAGMENTS. That is a concrete, objective confirmation, not a vacuous one.

3) The finding is self-refuting on "un-checkable": its own recommendation (a) — assert the seeded scan_roots/exemptions equal the source test's _SCAN_ROOTS/_EXCLUDED_PATH_FRAGMENTS — is a trivially-checkable proof, which demonstrates the checkpoint CAN be satisfied. A checkpoint for which a one-line equality assertion suffices is not "un-satisfiable."

4) This re-litigates a root fact already adjudicated. .gates/post-plan.json:125 refuted the sizing-angle version of the same observation, establishing that the WP1 retired_literal seed is a near-transcription of existing Python constants (NOT an investigative graph task) and that resolver/call_sites discovery is explicitly deferred (spec.md:33, C-002; call_sites? optional in the schema). The current finding just re-frames that settled fact as a "reviewer checkpoint" defect; it fails for the same reason.

Residual (why low, not dismissed outright): there is a genuine but minor wording imprecision. C-003 (spec.md:60) and the WP01 reviewer line (WP01:67) use the compressed word "discovered" and name the import-graph resolver without stating it applies only to the symbol/call-site kinds — for the retired_literal seed the honest description is "a reviewed transcription of the superseded test's proven scan scope." A one-line reconciliation (finding's rec (a)), optionally mechanized as an equality assertion, would improve DIR-003 traceability. That is a low-severity spec-polish nit consistent with how the sibling post-plan gate findings were adjudicated (all real=false, downgraded to low) — not a medium coherence defect and not an un-satisfiable checkpoint.

Relevant paths: kitty-specs/contract-ownership-boundary-01KWYRE5/{spec.md (C-003:60, FR-004:43, edge cases 29-33, assumptions 84), design.md (116,118,101-102,160,200,203), tasks/WP01-contract-registry-model.md (T003:59, reviewer:67), tasks/WP03-parity-prove-literal-sweeps.md, .gates/post-plan.json:125}; tests/architectural/test_no_dead_symbols.py; tests/architectural/test_no_legacy_terminology.py.

dropped.summary: issue-matrix.md is entirely placeholder including the 'Closes #2441' row, with no WP designated to reach a terminal verdict
dropped.why_refuted: The finding's literal quotes are accurate but its conclusion collapses under scrutiny. (1) issue-matrix.md:7-9 is the byte-for-byte, unmodified output of the canonical scaffolder src/specify_cli/tasks/issue_matrix.py::scaffold_issue_matrix(), which writes exactly `| #{n} | <fill at WP-implementation time> | unknown | <link or commit> |` during `spec-kitty agent mission finalize-tasks`. The placeholder title itself says the fill happens "at WP-implementation time." (2) The mission is at post-tasks / pre-implementation: status.events.jsonl ends at TasksCompleted and status.json shows zero WPs in any active lane, so the deferred fill correctly has not run yet — the finding flags an auto-generated scaffold for being in its designed state at the exact phase the tooling prescribes. (3) The finding's stated risk ("mission-done cannot be reached with unknown verdicts") is already closed by a hard gate it itself references: spec-kitty-mission-review/SKILL.md:593-595 makes any `unknown` verdict a HARD FAIL and rejects any surviving `in-mission` row on the `done` transition. The scaffold-now / fill-at-implementation / hard-gate-at-review lifecycle is by design; there is no path to mission-done with unknown verdicts. (4) The recommendation is partly incorrect and would bake in wrong verdicts if applied now: spec.md:4 declares #2077 and #2438 as Refs (not Closes; #2438 is explicitly "later"/deferred), so assigning #2077 an `in-mission` verdict — which must reach a terminal verdict before done — would obligate this MVP to close a reference-only issue, forcing scope creep or a Gate-4 fail. Per the skill, `in-mission` evidence cannot exist until the WP lands, so relabeling unknown→in-mission at planning time adds no coverage beyond what Gate-4 already enforces. Row coverage is in fact complete (one row per referenced issue); only the intentionally-deferred fill is absent. Net: an expected, canonical, gate-protected planning-phase state misread as a coverage/traceability defect, with a remediation that would introduce incorrect verdicts. Not a real finding.
